items.title: French troops complete withdrawal from Chad after 70 years | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>French troops formally completed their withdrawal from Chad, marking the end of France's military presence in the central African nation after nearly 70 years. 

#france #chad #africa #centralafrica #troops #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Congo teacher among hundreds joining the fight against M23 | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>In Democratic Republic of Congo's Bukavu, Mushagalusa Habamungu is one of hundreds to join the fight to defend his city. Hundreds have joined former soldier and local MP Albert Kahasha to reinforce the Congolese armed forces against the M23 rebels.

#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #congo #M23 #bukavu

items.title: How greasy fur helps polar bears brave the cold | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Scientists identified the unique anti-icing properties of polar bear fur, attributing its effectiveness to a natural grease coating. The discovery helps explain how the species endures Arctic conditions and suggests potential uses in industrial anti-icing technologies.

#polarbear #science #arctic #News #Reuters #Newsfeed

items.title: Trump to impose tariffs on Canada, Mexico, China on Saturday | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>US President Donald Trump said he will implement 25% tariffs against Canada and Mexico, and 10% on Chinese goods. Trump's order and retaliatory tariffs from Canada and Mexico could disrupt nearly $1.6 trillion in North American trade.

#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #donaldtrump #canada #mexico #china

items.title: LIVE: NTSB on DC helicopter-plane midair crash
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>The National Transportation Safety Board holds a briefing as recovery efforts continue following a midair collision between a passenger jet and a military helicopter at a Washington airport. All 67 people involved were killed.
 
#Crash #PlaneCrash #Helicopter #Washington #Airport #ReaganWashingtonNationalAirport #NTSB #Reuters #News #Live

items.title: US to levy tariffs on Mexico, Canada, China
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt said the United States will impose 25% tariffs on Canada and Mexico, along with a 10% tariff on China on February 1, but declined to say whether there will be exemptions.
 
President Trump's punitive duties and retaliatory tariffs from Canada and Mexico threaten to disrupt nearly $1.6 trillion in North American trade and effectively end a 30-year free trade system that has deeply integrated the three economies.
 
#UnitedStates #Canada #Mexico #Tariffs #Reuters #News

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>DC officials speak to reporters after a military helicopter collided with an American Airlines regional plane near D.C.'s Reagan National Airport, killing everyone on board. 
 
#Crash #PlaneCrash #Helicopter #BlackHawkHelicopter #Washington #AmericanAirlines

items.title: LIVE: White House briefing with Karoline Leavitt
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>White House press secretary Karoline Leavitt holds a briefing as North America braces for new Trump tariffs. The U.S. president is expected to announce new tariffs against Canada and Mexico that will begin on March 1, but will include a process for the countries to seek specific exemptions for certain imports, three people familiar with the planning told Reuters.
 
#WhiteHouse #KarolineLeavitt #Trump #DonaldTrump #USPolitics #Canada #Mexico #Politics #Reuters #News #Live

Keep up with the latest news from around the world: https://www.reuters.com/</article>

items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Acting U.S. Attorney for the Central District of California Joseph T. McNally and Akil Davis, FBI Assistant Director in Charge of the Los Angeles Field Office, hold a briefing on the investigation into an incident during the Palisades Fire that scorched part of Los Angeles.

items.title: UK rappers open new supermarket to help minorities 'feel seen' | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>Award-winning rap duo Casyo Johnson and Karl Wilson are set to open a new supermarket to help ethnic minorities ‘feel seen.’ Shoppers will be able to bulk-buy halal meat, exotic fish and fruits, as well as ethnic hair and beauty products.
 
#News #Reuters #Newsfeed #UK #rappers #supermarket

items.title: 'Grandma, protect yourself:' the Kenyan women fighting femicide | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>From the young woman brutally murdered and dismembered in a short-term rental apartment to the Olympic runner set on fire by her estranged boyfriend, a surge in violence against women in Kenya has spurred many to prepare themselves to fight back.

items.title: Key US inflation measure posts largest monthly gain since last April | REUTERS
items.author: Reuters
items.content:
<article>US inflation increased by the most in eight months in December amid a surge in consumer spending, suggesting the Federal Reserve would probably be in no hurry to resume cutting interest rates soon.
